revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=242 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:35:36.242606+00 | translation moa=243 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:35:40.220262+00

p.cl: Vniuersis sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus ⁊ futuris . Walter . miseratione diuina ecclesie Glasg’ minister eternam in domino . salutem.

Nouerit uniuersitas uestra nos de consensu decani et
capituli Glasguensis . caritatis intuitu dedisse et concessisse . et hac presenti carta nostra confirmasse . deo et Ecclesie Sancte Crucis de Edinburc . et Canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus et seruituris
Quod de quatuor ecclesiis quas habent in diocesi nostra sub hanc forma imperpetuum taxentur uicarie . scilicet . de ecclesia de kyrkecok  percipiat uicarius decem marcas in altaragio eiusdem
ecclesie sibi assignandas per visum magistri . H . Archidiaconi . Glasg’. et Magistrorum . Ricardi de Brade . et Ricardi de Harhall’ . clericorum nostrorum . De ecclesia uero de Crauford percipiat uicarius centum
solidos in altaragio eiusdum ecclesie . sibi assignandos per eosdem . De ecclesia uero de dalgernoc  percipiat uicarius decem marcas in bonis altaragii eiusdem ecclesie sibi assignandas per eosdem . In ecclesia
uero sancte Brigide de Loublaket  percipiat uicarius centum solidos  in altaragio eiusdem ecclesie sibi assignandos per eosdem . vicarii uero dictarum ecclesiarum de Episcopalibus et honoribus ordinariis et consue
tis dictas ecclesias contingentibus respondeant . De extra ordinariis autem abbas et conuentus dicte ecclesie sancte Crucis de Edenburc . Et dictarum ecclesiarum uicarii pro porcionibus in suprascriptis
ecclesiis se contingentibus  equaliter respondeant . Et cum aliqua dictarum ecclesiarum uacauerit  concessimus supra dictis Canonicis ut eam sine aliqua contradiccione et difficultate ingrediantur .
eamque in usus proprios  pro uoluntate sua conuertant . salua nobis et successoribus custodia uicariarum cum uacauerint donec cappellanum ydoneum uel canonicum si maluerint Iuxta canonicas sanc
ciones nobis ad easdem presentauerint Qui dictam uicariam dum caste et honeste se habuerit  tempore suo possideat

IN huius autem rei testimonium et ut ista concessio rata et
firma maneat inperpetuum presens scriptum sigillo nostro signamus.

Hiis Testibus Magistro . H . Archidiacono Glasg’ . Magistris R . et R . de Brade ⁊ de Harhall’
Magistro symon medico de Bygre . Domino . Ric’ . cappellano ⁊ uicario de Galsg’ . Domino . Ric . vicario de Dunoglas . Domino Waltero clerico de Ledemere . Et aliis .
To all sons of Holy Mother Church, present and future, Walter, by divine mercy, servant of the church of Glasgow, wishes eternal salvation in the Lord:

May you all be aware that we, with the agreement of the dean and chapter of Glasgow, and moved by charity, have given and granted, and by this present charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there now and in the future, that in respect of the four churches that they have in our diocese, the vicarages should be valued according to this pattern in perpetuity, that is, for the church of Kyrkecok [Urr], the vicar should receive ten merks as the altarage of the said church, assigned to him by the inspection of Master Hugh, archdeacon of Glasgow, and Masters Richard of Braid and Richard of Arnhall, our clerks; for the church of Crawford, the vicar should receive a hundred shillings as the altarge of the said church, assigned to him by the same men; for the church of Dalgarnock, the vicar should receive ten merks as goods of the altarge of the same church, assigned to him by the same men; in the church of St Bride at Blaiket [Kirkbride], the vicar should receive a hundred shillings as altarage of the same church, assigned to him by the same men; the vicars of the said churches should answer for episcopal dues, ordinary fees and customs relating to the said churches, but for extraordinary dues, the abbot and convent of the said church of Holyrood in Edinburgh; and the vicars of the said churches should answer equally for the portions relating to them in the churches mentioned above. And when any of the said churches is vacant, we have granted to the said canons that they should enter it without any objection and difficulty, and turn it to their use as they please, reserving to ourself and our successors the wardship of the vicarages when they become vacant until they have presented us with a suitable chaplain to the same, or a canon if they prefer, according to the canonical precepts; and while he possesses the said vicarage, he should during his time keep himself chaste and honest.
In testimony of this matter and that this grant remain certain and stable in perpetuity, we have sealed the present document with out seal.

With these witnesses, Master Hugh, archdeacon of Glasgow; Masters Richard and Richard of Braid and of Arnhall; Master Simon the physician of Biggar; the lord Richard, chaplain and vicar of Glasgow; the lord Richard, vicar of Douglas; the lord Walter, clerk of Ledemere; and others.
